identity.canonical_title: BIOLOGICAL HILBERT DIMENSIONALITY Canonical Statutory Formula: dim(H_bio)

theory.canonical_statement: [ID 1178] BIOLOGICAL HILBERT DIMENSIONALITY Canonical Statutory Formula: $\dim(\mathcal{H}_{bio}) = 4^N$ Formal Technical Dissertation: Establishes the source category for the information manifold. The total state space of the biological operator is defined as a Hilbert space of dimension $4^N$ (where $N$ is the sequence length), representing the maximal potential information content of the sequence. Source: [3]
theory.technical_interpretation: BIOLOGICAL HILBERT DIMENSIONALITY Canonical Statutory Formula: dim(H_bio) is an algebraic definition, written dim(H_bio) = 4^N. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from N, defining dim(H_bio). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
